steps.title: Match the claim about fear
steps.content: The claim is not just that a sailor came to the inn. It says the narrator was frightened by the imagined one-legged seafaring man, so the best quotation must show fear, haunting, or emotional disturbance from the narrator's point of view.

steps.title: Compare the quotations
steps.content: Choice A describes the seafaring man haunting the narrator's dreams and appearing in frightening forms on stormy nights, which directly illustrates fear. Choice B only recalls the sailor's arrival, Choice C describes his silent habits near the cove, and Choice D describes clothing and a hat. Those neutral details do not show the narrator being frightened. The answer is A.
